title: Astragalus membranaceus (Astragalus)
intro: Astragalus membranaceus (Astragalus) is a medicinal herb that has been used for thousands of years in traditional Chinese medicine to strengthen the immune system and reduce inflammation. The active constituents of Astragalus include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and triterpenes. It is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in supplement form. While generally safe, Astragalus may cause side effects such as upset stomach, diarrhea, and dry mouth in some individuals. Those with certain medical conditions, such as diabetes or kidney disease, should consult their healthcare provider before using Astragalus.
